slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison

slides.speaker_notes: Export kind: policy_contract_comparison_deck. Deck family: comparison. Sources: structured_result, document_agent_comparison.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log serves as a control evidence tracker for vendor reviews, with closure contingent on resolving missing or pending artifacts—particularly EV-002—highlighting operational gaps in evidence completeness. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email outlines a conditional, time-bound governance control requiring strict scoping, logging, quarterly reviews, and personnel verification to maintain access, emphasizing proactive accountability rather than reactive evidence resolution.

- Control closure in the Access Review Evidence Log depends on verifying incomplete or pending evidence, directly impacting control status.  
- The Privileged Account Approval Email mandates ongoing governance conditions, including quarterly reviews and personnel revalidation, to sustain access.  
- Evidence gaps in the Access Review Log affect immediate control closure, while the approval email requires continuous compliance to prevent access drift.  
- The Access Review Evidence Log is synthetic and for demonstration only; the approval email reflects a live, conditional governance process.  
- Missing evidence in the Access Review Log is a tactical gap; the approval email enforces strategic governance through structured accountability.  
- Control ownership differs: the Access Review Log assigns ownership to evidence verification, whereas the approval email ties ownership to personnel and audit adherence.
